title: Eria ferox
intro: Eria ferox, also known as the "Wild Orchid," is a species of flowering plant in the orchid family (Orchidaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine, and its health benefits include its ability to treat fever, reduce inflammation, and aid in digestion. The bioactive constituents of Eria ferox include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In herbal medicine, Eria ferox is often prepared as a powder or decoction, and is used to make various remedies such as teas, capsules, and ointments.
taxonomy: Eria ferox is a species of orchid native to the Himalayas. Eria ferox belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This genus Eria is part of the subfamily Orchidoideae. Eria ferox is a terrestrial orchid, which means it grows in the ground. It is a relatively small orchid species compared to others in the same family.
